findings.title: Sensitive file exposed: /.env
findings.description: Source-control metadata or environment file is publicly readable, frequently leaking credentials and internal structure.
findings.remediation: Block these paths at the edge and rotate any leaked secrets.

findings.title: Sensitive port 6379/Redis reachable from the internet
findings.description: Redis is exposed publicly. Data stores and admin protocols should never be internet-reachable; many ship with weak/no auth by default.
findings.remediation: Restrict via security group/NACL to known CIDRs or a bastion; bind to private subnets.

findings.title: Sensitive port 9200/Elasticsearch reachable from the internet
findings.description: Elasticsearch is exposed publicly. Data stores and admin protocols should never be internet-reachable; many ship with weak/no auth by default.
findings.remediation: Restrict via security group/NACL to known CIDRs or a bastion; bind to private subnets.

findings.title: Deprecated TLS version accepted
findings.description: Server negotiates legacy TLS, which has known cryptographic weaknesses.

findings.title: Admin/management surface exposed: /actuator/env
findings.description: An administrative interface is reachable from the internet without an auth gate.
findings.remediation: Restrict to VPN/SSO/allow-list; never expose admin panels publicly.

findings.title: Dangerous HTTP methods enabled: PUT, DELETE, TRACE
findings.description: Write/diagnostic verbs are enabled; PUT/DELETE may allow content tampering, TRACE can enable Cross-Site Tracing.
findings.remediation: Disable unused methods at the application/proxy layer.

findings.title: HSTS not set
findings.description: Without HSTS, clients can be downgraded to HTTP and are vulnerable to SSL-stripping man-in-the-middle attacks.

findings.title: TLS certificate expiring soon
findings.remediation: Renew now; ensure automated rotation is in place.

findings.title: API documentation exposed: /v3/api-docs
findings.description: Swagger/OpenAPI specs map the full attack surface for an attacker.
findings.remediation: Disable docs in production or require authentication.

findings.title: Internet-facing hostname suggests non-public service
findings.description: The hostname contains keywords that usually denote internal/admin/non-prod systems that should not be publicly resolvable.
findings.remediation: Confirm this must be public; otherwise move behind VPN/private DNS.

findings.title: Sensitive port 22/SSH reachable from the internet
findings.description: SSH is exposed publicly. Data stores and admin protocols should never be internet-reachable; many ship with weak/no auth by default.
findings.remediation: Restrict via security group/NACL to known CIDRs or a bastion; bind to private subnets.

findings.title: Content-Security-Policy not set
findings.description: No CSP increases the blast radius of any XSS on the asset.
findings.remediation: Define a restrictive CSP appropriate to the app.

findings.title: X-Content-Type-Options not set
findings.description: MIME sniffing can turn benign uploads into script execution.

findings.title: Clickjacking protection missing
findings.description: Neither X-Frame-Options nor a CSP frame-ancestors directive is present.

findings.title: Server version disclosed in banner
findings.description: The Server header leaks software and version, aiding targeted exploitation.
findings.remediation: Suppress or genericise the Server header at the proxy.

findings.title: No WAF/CDN detected in front of public web asset
findings.description: No edge protection signature was observed; the origin may be directly exposed to L7 attacks and volumetric traffic.
findings.remediation: Front the asset with AWS WAF + CloudFront or equivalent.

review.summary: 16 issue(s) found on api-internal.acme-corp.com (dns_record, owner: payments-team). Highest severity: CRITICAL — Sensitive file exposed: /.env.
key_findings: [CRITICAL/MEDIUM conf] Sensitive port 6379/Redis reachable from the internet
[CRITICAL/MEDIUM conf] Sensitive port 9200/Elasticsearch reachable from the internet
[HIGH/MEDIUM conf] Dangerous HTTP methods enabled: PUT, DELETE, TRACE
review.potential_impact: Source-control metadata or environment file is publicly readable, frequently leaking credentials and internal structure.
recommended_actions: Block these paths at the edge and rotate any leaked secrets.
Restrict via security group/NACL to known CIDRs or a bastion; bind to private subnets.
Restrict to VPN/SSO/allow-list; never expose admin panels publicly.
Disable unused methods at the application/proxy layer.
Renew now; ensure automated rotation is in place.
Disable docs in production or require authentication.
Confirm this must be public; otherwise move behind VPN/private DNS.
